queries.query: What was the legal issue decided by the Supreme Court regarding the starting point of limitation for filing objections to an arbitral award under the Arbitration Act, 1940?
Which specific provision of the Limitation Act, 1963 governs the time period for filing objections to an arbitral award, and what is the prescribed limitation period?
On which date did the Supreme Court hold that the respondent became aware of the arbitral award for the purpose of calculating limitation?
Why did the Supreme Court conclude that the application filed under Section 17 of the Arbitration Act, 1940 was not premature in this case?

How has the Supreme Court consistently interpreted the expression “notice of the filing of the award” under Section 14(2) of the Arbitration Act, 1940 across multiple decisions, and does it mandate a formal written notice in all cases?
